title: Ischyrolepis distracta

taxonomy: Ischyrolepis distracta is classified as a species of plant in the family Restionaceae, commonly known as the restio or club-rush family. This family consists of small to medium-sized plants that are often found in wetland and coastal areas. Ischyrolepis distracta is specifically a genus of plants found in Southern Africa. The classification of Ischyrolepis distracta is further detailed within the monocotyledons division of plants, which includes a wide range of grass-like and flowering plants. This division is characterized by having one cotyledon in the seedling stage.
uses: Ischyrolepis distracta has traditionally been used in South African folk medicine, though its use in contemporary medicine is limited.
